original_hypothesis.title: release() Fails to Update userTotalStaked — Permanent Fund Lockup After Slash Appeal
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The release() function restores slashedAmount back to stake.amount but never increments userTotalStaked[staker]. Since slash() decrements userTotalStaked, and release() does not reverse this, a permanent accounting desync is created. When the user later calls withdrawSelfStake or withdrawCommunityStake, the line `userTotalStaked[msg.sender] -= amount` will underflow (Solidity 0.8.x checked arithmetic) and revert, permanently locking the released tokens in the contract.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a demonstrable code bug that breaks the protocol's stated invariant (userTotalStaked consistency). Every successful slash+release cycle permanently locks the released amount. This directly contradicts the protocol's appeal mechanism design intent.

proof_of_signal.pattern: release() modifies selfStakes[staker].amount += amountToRelease and communityStakes[staker][stakee].amount += amountToRelease but NEVER executes userTotalStaked[staker] += amountToRelease. Compare with slash() at lines 276-310 which DOES execute userTotalStaked[staker] -= slashedAmount.

preconditions: User has an active self-stake or community-stake
SLASHER_ROLE slashes the user (any percent, any amount > 0)
RELEASER_ROLE releases any portion of the slashed amount
User's lock period expires and they attempt withdrawal

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: slash: userTotalStaked[staker] -= slashedAmount; release: [missing] userTotalStaked[staker] += amountToRelease
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Any slash+release creates a permanent deficit: userTotalStaked < sum(stake.amounts). The deficit equals the total amount ever released.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No attacker profit — this is a fund lockup bug. Victim loses the released amount permanently. For each release of X tokens, X tokens become permanently unwithdrawable.

economic_questions: What is the total value at risk across all users who have been slashed and released?
Can affected users work around the bug by adding more stake to 'top up' userTotalStaked?
Does repeated slash+release compound the deficit?
validation_requirements: Trace: selfStake(100) → slash(50%) → release(50) → withdrawSelfStake(100) and confirm revert
Verify workaround: after release, user stakes 50 more (userTotalStaked=100), can withdraw 100 but remaining 50 is still locked
Confirm both self-stake and community-stake release paths are affected

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: N/A — this is a protocol bug, not an attack requiring capital
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: N/A — no attacker profit; victim loss equals released amount
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Permanent — locked tokens cannot be recovered without contract upgrade

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Triggers any time a slash is followed by a release
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Proportional to total slashed-and-released GTC across all protocol users

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Any staker who has been slashed and subsequently had funds released via appeal
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% of the released amount is permanently unwithdrawable
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All GTC that passes through the slash→release lifecycle

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Track userTotalStaked vs sum(stake.amounts) after release()
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify underflow revert on withdrawal after release
investigation_priorities.execution_complexity: low — automatic trigger on normal protocol operations
investigation_priorities.detection_difficulty: low — any post-release withdrawal attempt reveals the bug

validation_result.impact_summary: release() restores stake.amount but never increments userTotalStaked, causing permanent fund lockup when the user attempts withdrawal due to arithmetic underflow revert in Solidity 0.8.x.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The release() function in IdentityStaking.sol restores slashed tokens back to the stake's amount field but omits the corresponding update to userTotalStaked[staker]. Since slash() decrements userTotalStaked and release() does not re-increment it, a permanent accounting desynchronization is created. When the user later attempts to withdraw via withdrawSelfStake or withdrawCommunityStake, the line `userTotalStaked[msg.sender] -= amount` will underflow (since userTotalStaked is less than the actual stake amount) and revert under Solidity 0.8.x checked arithmetic. This permanently locks the released tokens in the contract.

Trace: selfStake(100) → userTotalStaked=100, amount=100. slash(50%) → userTotalStaked=50, amount=50, slashedAmount=50. release(50) → userTotalStaked=50 (UNCHANGED), amount=100, slashedAmount=0. withdrawSelfStake(100) → amount check passes (100<=100), but userTotalStaked[msg.sender] -= 100 causes 50-100 underflow → REVERT. User can only withdraw 50 of their 100 GTC.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal is accurate. The release() function modifies selfStakes[staker].amount and communityStakes[staker][stakee].amount but never touches userTotalStaked. The slash() function correctly decrements userTotalStaked but release() fails to reverse this decrement. The withdrawal functions both subtract from userTotalStaked, which will underflow after a slash+release cycle.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: release() increments stake.amount by amountToRelease but never increments userTotalStaked[staker]. Since slash() decremented userTotalStaked, this creates a permanent deficit. Both the self-stake and community-stake paths are affected.
slash() correctly decrements both stake.amount and userTotalStaked. The asymmetry is that release() increments stake.amount but does NOT increment userTotalStaked, creating the desync.

The line `userTotalStaked[msg.sender] -= amount` will underflow and revert when amount exceeds userTotalStaked (which happens after a slash+release cycle since userTotalStaked was not restored by release()).
Same underflow vulnerability as withdrawSelfStake for community stakes after a slash+release cycle.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls selfStake(100, duration): selfStakes[user].amount=100, userTotalStaked[user]=100
2. SLASHER calls slash([user], [], [], 50): selfStakes[user].amount=50, slashedAmount=50, userTotalStaked[user]=50
3. RELEASER calls release(user, user, 50, currentSlashRound): selfStakes[user].slashedAmount=0, selfStakes[user].amount=100, userTotalStaked[user]=50 (NOT UPDATED)
4. Time passes, unlock expires. User calls withdrawSelfStake(100): amount check passes (100<=100), but userTotalStaked[user] -= 100 causes 50-100 = UNDERFLOW REVERT
5. User can only withdraw up to 50. The remaining 50 GTC is permanently locked.
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The defect is in release() which modifies stake.amount (lines ~375-376 for self-stake, lines ~385-386 for community-stake) but never modifies userTotalStaked. Compare with slash() which correctly modifies both stake.amount (line ~308) and userTotalStaked (line ~310). The withdrawal functions (withdrawSelfStake line ~228, withdrawCommunityStake line ~283) subtract from userTotalStaked, which will underflow when userTotalStaked < stake.amount due to the release() bug.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided for IdentityStaking. The deploy.ts script deploys a 'Lock' contract (unrelated). The slash.ts script is a dummy placeholder for EAS attestation, not actual slashing.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attack requiring adversarial setup — it is a protocol-level bug triggered by normal operations. Scenario: (1) Any user stakes GTC via selfStake or communityStake. (2) SLASHER_ROLE slashes the user for any percentage > 0. (3) RELEASER_ROLE releases any portion of the slashed amount (e.g., after a successful appeal). (4) User's lock period expires and they attempt full withdrawal. (5) Withdrawal reverts due to underflow in `userTotalStaked[msg.sender] -= amount`. The released amount (step 3) is permanently unwithdrawable. Both self-stake and community-stake paths are affected. The bug compounds: every slash+release cycle increases the deficit.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL. Every successful slash+release cycle permanently locks the released token amount. The deficit equals the cumulative total of all tokens ever released via the release() function. Affected users lose 100% of the released amount — it becomes permanently unwithdrawable. The only workaround is for affected users to add additional stake to 'top up' userTotalStaked, but this requires spending additional tokens and the topped-up amount itself becomes at risk. A contract upgrade is required to fix the desynchronization. This directly undermines the protocol's appeal mechanism, as releasing slashed funds (the intended outcome of a successful appeal) actually harms the staker by locking their tokens.
recommended_mitigations: Add `userTotalStaked[staker] += amountToRelease;` in both the self-stake and community-stake branches of the release() function
Add a comprehensive invariant test that verifies userTotalStaked[addr] == selfStakes[addr].amount + sum(communityStakes[addr][*].amount) after every state-modifying operation
Consider adding an admin function to fix existing userTotalStaked desynchronizations for any users already affected before the fix is deployed
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy IdentityStaking behind UUPS proxy. 2. Initialize with GTC token, burn address, admin, slasher, releaser. 3. User approves and calls selfStake(100e18, 12 weeks). 4. Verify userTotalStaked[user] == 100e18. 5. Slasher calls slash([user], [], [], 50). 6. Verify userTotalStaked[user] == 50e18, selfStakes[user].amount == 50e18. 7. Releaser calls release(user, user, 50e18, currentSlashRound). 8. Verify selfStakes[user].amount == 100e18 but userTotalStaked[user] == 50e18 (BUG). 9. Advance time past unlockTime. 10. User calls withdrawSelfStake(100e18) → expect REVERT due to underflow. 11. User calls withdrawSelfStake(51e18) → expect REVERT. 12. User calls withdrawSelfStake(50e18) → succeeds but 50e18 GTC permanently locked. 13. Repeat steps 3-12 with communityStake to confirm both paths affected.
additional_testing_needed: Verify whether any existing mainnet users have been affected by this bug (check if release() has ever been called on-chain)
Test compound effect: multiple slash+release cycles on the same user to confirm deficit accumulates
Test workaround: verify user can add more stake to increase userTotalStaked and partially recover
